message:
fix(fp-frame): Series.searchsorted accepts non-numeric needles (frankenpandas-yrhm)

The previous implementation called value.to_f64() unconditionally,
which rejected Utf8 and Bool needles with NonNumericValue even
though pandas happily accepts string and bool searchsorted on
string- or bool-sorted Series (e.g.
pd.Series(['a','b','c']).searchsorted('b') → 1).

Routes the comparison through a new compare_scalar_values helper
that dispatches on Scalar variant — Int64/i64::cmp,
Float64/partial_cmp, Utf8/str::cmp, Bool/bool::cmp,
Timedelta64/i64::cmp. Mixed-variant pairs fall back to numeric
comparison via to_f64 (Equal if either side is non-numeric), so
callers still get a best-effort answer when a type mismatch slips
through.

Side validation now up front: unknown sides and missing needles
return CompatibilityRejected with clear messages. The existing
series_searchsorted_values_reject_missing_value test is updated to
match the new clearer error variant.

Four new tests: utf8 searchsorted (left/right/boundary), bool
searchsorted, invalid-side rejection, missing-needle rejection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
